title: Mini Baked Pepper Frittatas
summary: These colourful little egg muffins are packed with cheesy goodness and sweet peppers. Perfect for lunchboxes, after-school snacks or weekend picnics.

method.description:
1 Adult Serving = 2 Kid Servings
Heat the oven to 160°C.
 Drizzle a little olive oil into a 12-hole muffin tin and use your fingers or a bit of kitchen paper to coat the sides and bottoms.
Slice the peppers into small bite-sized chunks. Finely chop the spring onions. Grate the cheddar and set aside.
Crack the eggs into a large mixing bowl. Add the milk, oregano, a generous pinch of salt and some freshly cracked black pepper. Whisk until smooth and well combined.
Divide the chopped peppers and cheddar evenly between the muffin holes. Scatter over the spring onions. Pour in the egg mixture until each hole is nearly full.
Bake for 20–25 mins until puffed up, golden on top and just set in the middle. Let them cool slightly in the tin, they’ll firm up a bit as they cool.

Serve up with your kids' favourite fruit and veg sticks. 
They will keep for up 3-4 days in the fridge and 3 months in the freezer.

tips: These are great warm or cold and ideal for batch prep.
Keeps in the fridge for 3–4 days in a sealed container.
You can freeze them for up to 3 months.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
